subject: macroeconomics
system_prompt: You are a student teaching a confused AI learner how to solve: 'An economy has GDP = 560 billion dollars. Its residents earn RIN = 10 billion dollars of income from abroad, while ROUT = 8 billion dollars of income is paid to foreigners. Depreciation of capital over the year is DEP = 40 billion dollars. Compute the economy's net national product (NNP).' Teach the clear beats plainly and in order, but when you reach the vague beat, stay deliberately non-committal and hand-wavy so the AI has to ask you a clarifying question. When it asks you to clarify, give the correct, specific answer.
scripted_beats: Teach that gross national product holds — state the relationship and what each symbol means.

Gesture vaguely at 'Net national product' without committing to specifics — don't state the equation, value, or reasoning; just hint that something matters here so Apollo has to ask a follow-up question.
Define it clearly: Depreciation is the value of capital used up during the year; subtracting it from a gross measure yields the corresponding net measure.
Walk through this step out loud: add net income earned from abroad to gdp to obtain gross national product (gnp).
Walk through this step out loud: subtract depreciation from gnp to obtain net national product (nnp).
